summary: Capture follow-ups from the sustained auto-improve workflow review.

observations.problem: Run failures were not captured as structured next-run decisions, so reviewer, validator, mutation, repo-health, or promotion-gate blockers could remain indirect calibration signals instead of explicit queue work.
observations.why_it_matters: True sustained self-improvement should first decide whether any failed run outcome is actionable next-run repair evidence; only carry it forward when bounded and otherwise let the queue choose other work.
observations.automation_candidate: Record next_run_decisions in the auto-improve session and let mutation-proposal generation convert open carry_forward decisions into priority next_run_failure_repair proposals before general discovery work.
observations.suggested_followup: Implemented generalized failure-to-next-run decision routing with tests for actionable carry-forward, retryable capacity closure, repair proposal emission, and closed-decision suppression.

observations.problem: readiness, session synopsis, and remediation backlog still converge through repeated tracked report writes, so stale inputs and dirty report churn can mask the actual self-improvement signal.
observations.why_it_matters: A long unattended loop should separate run-local state from canonical publish state and should have a deterministic one-command fixed point rather than requiring operators to learn a retry order.
observations.automation_candidate: Split preview/final readiness inputs or add a bounded fixed-point reconcile command that writes run-local candidates until fingerprints stabilize, then publishes canonical reports once.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated by goal-runtime-local-evidence-converge and goal-runtime-publish-local-evidence: readiness, session synopsis, negative lessons, and remediation backlog converge under runs/goal-$(GOAL_RUN_ID)/state, pass local fixed-point check, and publish canonical reports once before final fixed-point/index/freshness checks.

observations.problem: A previous auto-improve ladder process continued running with old 30-minute profile arguments and modified the same working tree while follow-up maintenance was underway.
observations.why_it_matters: Concurrent unattended runs can revert or race intentional operator edits, creating dirty-state failures that look like validation issues instead of process ownership conflicts.
observations.automation_candidate: Add a repository-wide goal ladder lock and stale PID inspection command that blocks new manual maintenance or new ladder starts while an active ladder process owns the workspace.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated by a workspace-wide goal runtime lock: runner acquisition blocks concurrent runs, long-run-preflight-clean and auto-improve-goal-preflight fail on active locks, stale locks are cleaned, and goal-runtime-lock-status/stop expose inspection plus explicit SIGTERM request targets.
